steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: Kean's interest in Parisi's collection centers on its focus on “false starts...and mistakes that beset real scientific research every day.” He argues that scientists must include this “human stuff” to properly explain how science “really gets done.”

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Kean's appreciation for Parisi's work reveals they share the same value: showing the messy human reality of science. The others either invent disagreement, confuse form with values, or misread what the passage discusses. That matches choice B — B is correct.
